headline: BONANZA GOLD: Cambria Drills 22m of 17.95 g/t at BC's Premier Mine, Including 0.5m of 552 g/t
snippet: Cambria Gold Mines intersected 22.0m averaging 17.95 g/t gold in infill drilling at the Premier Mine in BC's Golden Triangle, with a bonanza-grade 0.5m interval at 552 g/t — results from a 27,000m program designed to support a restart of the historic past-producing mine.

headline: $325M DEAL DONE: B2Gold and Agnico Eagle Close Fingold Sale, Seal Nunavut Collaboration Pact
snippet: B2Gold closed its US$325 million sale of a 70% interest in Fingold Ventures to Agnico Eagle, simultaneously entering a Nunavut Collaboration Agreement covering shared best practices in arctic mining, logistics, and environmental management across two of Canada's largest northern gold operations.

headline: U.S. URANIUM FLOWS: Ur-Energy Opens Second ISR Mine in Wyoming, Combined Capacity Now 4.2M Lbs/Yr
snippet: Ur-Energy announced uranium-bearing solution is being captured from Mine Unit 1 at Shirley Basin — its second Wyoming ISR project with 2.0M lb/yr licensed capacity and a nine-year mine life — bringing combined production capacity with Lost Creek to 4.2 million pounds U₃O₈ annually.

headline: GRASBERG GUTTED: Freeport Slashes Copper Forecast 27%, Shares Sink 8%; Morgan Stanley Downgrades
snippet: Freeport-McMoRan cut its 2026 Grasberg copper guidance from 1.1 billion to 800 million pounds after unexpected groundwater delayed Indonesia mine recovery to just 65% of capacity — sending shares down more than 8% and triggering a Morgan Stanley downgrade.

headline: GIANT LITHIUM BET: Q2 Metals Drops 295Mt Cisco Resource — One of the Largest Hard-Rock Deposits in the Americas
snippet: Q2 Metals' inaugural resource estimate for Cisco in Quebec's James Bay pegs 295 million tonnes at 1.36% Li₂O Inferred — a 1.8-km continuous spodumene pegmatite body 6.5 km from the Billy Diamond Highway — which the Northern Miner ranks among the world's largest hard-rock lithium deposits.

headline: CONTANGO GOES BIG: $63M Alaska-BC Drill Program Targets 18,000m at Lucky Shot, $25M at Kitsault Valley
snippet: Contango Silver & Gold outlined a $63M 2026 exploration program spanning Alaska and northern BC, headlined by 18,000m of drilling at Lucky Shot near Anchorage and a $25M infill program at Kitsault Valley aimed at completing a preliminary economic assessment.

headline: GOLD AT $4,724, COPPER SLIDES TO $6.02: Asian Metals Digest as Markets Mark Time Before April 29 Fed
snippet: Asian session metals wrap shows gold at $4,723.60 (up 0.54%), copper retreating to $6.02/lb (down 0.99%), and silver consolidating near $77 — with Chinese pre-holiday restocking limiting copper's downside as traders position ahead of next week's FOMC decision.

headline: 99.5% FED HOLD PRICED IN: What Gold Investors Should Actually Watch in Powell's April 29 Statement
snippet: CME FedWatch sits at 99.5% probability the Fed holds at 3.50–3.75%, making the rate decision itself irrelevant — the gold trade is entirely about whether Powell signals openness to later 2026 cuts, which hinges on whether Strait of Hormuz developments ease oil-driven inflation expectations.

headline: SIX STRAIGHT: Silver Holds $76.88/oz as Analysts Project Record Sixth Consecutive Supply Deficit
snippet: Silver was priced at $76.88/oz on April 23 — up more than 150% year-on-year — with analysts forecasting a sixth straight structural supply deficit in 2026 as solar, industrial, and investment demand continues to outpace mine production growth projected to top 1.05 billion ounces.

headline: RATIO REVERSAL FLASHES BUY: Gold-Silver Ratio Contracts From 105+ — A Historically Reliable Silver Signal
snippet: The gold-to-silver ratio peaked above 105 in April before beginning to contract — a pattern that has historically preceded sharp silver outperformance, with past reversals from extreme levels delivering outsized gains for both silver and silver mining equities.

headline: STRUCTURAL TAILWINDS ALIGN: Gold, Silver and Copper All Face Tightening Supply as the Macro Setup Builds
snippet: An April 23 market commentary lays out the synchronized bull case across metals: six consecutive silver supply deficits, copper's triple demand surge from AI, defense, and energy transition, and central bank gold accumulation running at 850 tonnes projected for 2026 — all pointing to sustained price support.
